nodes.title: User Made Loras
widgets_values: If using some user made LTX-2 loras they sometimes are not trained on audio, so it will produce very noisy audio outputs. Try use KJNodes LTX-2 Lora Loader Advanced in such cases, and set the non video strenght to zero

nodes.title: Sampler Preview
widgets_values:
Tiny VAE for sampler previews. 
Optionally you can drop this for a lower res latent RGB built into the node 

https://github.com/madebyollin/taehv/blob/main/safetensors/taeltx2_3.safetensors

nodes.title: About Size
widgets_values:
Width & height settings must be divisible by 32 + 1. 
Frame count must be divisible by 8 + 1. 


Running with invalid parameters **will not cause errors**. Instead, the flow will silently choose the closest valid parameters. 

By default, we are using 720p resolution. You can try 1920*1088 if you have a powerful GPU.

nodes.title: LTXV Spatio Temporal Tiled VAE Decode
Temporal Upscale
widgets_values:
Entirely optional. It will double the FPS for a more smooth video (24 =>  48fps). 
But sometimes 24fps might be preferable for cinematic feel.

Its also quite slow, might be better to use other frame interpolation nodes if you want to. But included it so you can try out the LTX-2 temporal upscale as well

nodes.title: Prompting LTX-2
widgets_values:
## LTX-2 Prompting Tips
1. **Core Actions**: Describe events and actions as they occur over time  
2. **Audio**: Describe sounds and dialogue needed for the scene  
3. **Reference Image**: Do not repeat details already present  
4. **Consistency**: Avoid instructions that do not match the reference image, as this will degrade results

nodes.title: About  Models
widgets_values:
Download models from here:


https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy

Text encoder : https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/ltx-2

nodes.title: Video settings
widgets_values:
If low on Ram/Vram, try width height to 832 x 480 or 960 x 544.  If you can, run at 1280 x 720 or higher.


Length in seconds :  try 5, 10 or 20. 
fps : 24 or 25 (or 48 or 50 if your pc can run it)

exposed mechanical plates on top of skin, detachable armor pieces, floating
cybernetics, mask-like face plates, clean mechanical seams, cosplay prosthetics

[0s-4s] A cinematic medium shot in a bright, sun-baked desert with rolling sand dunes. A female cyborg stands in the center, initially facing away towards a rusted, blurred buggy in the background. She slowly pivots her feet in the deep sand, turning around to face the camera. As she moves, her heavy, distressed brown leather jacket aggressively crinkles and shifts. Her fully mechanical, silver left arm swings slightly, exposing complex internal wiring and sliding steel pistons. She grips a small metal key in her human right hand.
[4s-8s] The camera continuously and smoothly pushes in, closing the distance into a tight, dramatic close-up of her face as she completes her turn. The extreme detail of her split face is revealed: the right side features realistic, fine human skin and loose hair blowing in the wind, while the left side exposes dark metallic skull plating, neck wires, and a brightly glowing green mechanical optic. She stares intensely into the lens, the aperture of her glowing green eye physically dilating and shifting focus.
Audio:
[0s-4s] The dry, continuous howl of the desert wind. The soft, heavy crunch of boots shifting in deep sand, mixed with the tight, stiff creaking of heavy leather stretching.
[4s-8s] The wind noise drops slightly as the camera gets closer. A sharp, high-pitched mechanical whirring and a rapid sequence of high-tech microscopic clicks ring out as her cybernetic eye lens physically adjusts and locks focus.
